skill.name: global-conventions
skill.description: Apply project-wide conventions for repository structure, Git workflow, documentation, environment configuration, dependencies, reviews, and release notes.
skill.summary: Keep project organization and team workflow decisions consistent across the development lifecycle.
skill.author: EIS-ITS

security_audit.summary: The static path traversal findings on SKILL.md line 29 are semantically relevant because the skill points to a file outside its own package. No malicious intent or prompt injection was found, but publication should warn users that the skill depends on external repository-local documentation. The .env and weak-cryptography matches are false positives caused by descriptive text, not executable behavior.

medium_findings.title: Out-of-Package Relative File Reference
medium_findings.description: SKILL.md line 29 tells the assistant to refer to ../../../agent-os/standards/global/conventions.md. This upward relative path is not confirmed malicious, but it can make the skill depend on files outside the packaged skill directory and may expose unintended local documentation if resolved in a broader workspace.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The upward relative path is directly present and intentionally referenced as required guidance. Confidence is below very high because the target appears to be repository documentation, not a secret file or executable payload.

low_findings.title: Environment File Static Match Is Descriptive
low_findings.description: SKILL.md line 16 mentions working with .env files and secrets management as a use case. No evidence found that the skill reads, copies, uploads, or exposes environment files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is a plain-language scope statement. The reviewed file contains no commands, scripts, network calls, or instructions to reveal secret values.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Matches Are Text Only
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported weak cryptography matches on SKILL.md lines 3 and 24. The file contains descriptive skill metadata and prose, with no cryptographic API usage or hashing implementation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matched lines are natural-language descriptions. No evidence found of MD5, SHA-1, DES, or other weak cryptographic code in the scanned file.

dangerous_patterns.title: Upward Relative Path in Skill Instructions
dangerous_patterns.description: The instruction link on SKILL.md line 29 uses ../../../ to reach outside the skill directory. This is a filesystem risk pattern even when the intended target is documentation.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The path traversal sequence is explicit and repeated in the referenced link. The risk is contextual rather than confirmed malicious.

content.value_statement: Teams lose time when repository structure, Git practices, and documentation rules are inconsistent. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to apply shared project conventions during routine development work.
actual_capabilities: Guides decisions about project files, directories, and repository organization.
Supports consistent commit messages, branch workflows, and pull request preparation.
Helps update README files, changelogs, contribution notes, and setup documentation.
Applies conventions for environment variables, settings files, and secret handling.
Supports dependency file changes and feature flag documentation.
Promotes consistent testing and code review expectations before merging.
limitations: The skill references an external repository-local conventions file that is not included in this package.
It provides process guidance, not automated enforcement or policy validation.
It does not define detailed language-specific coding standards.
It does not replace human review for secrets, release readiness, or security decisions.

use_cases.title: Organize a New Repository
use_cases.description: Apply consistent folders, documentation, dependency files, and setup notes before a team starts implementation.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Pull Request
use_cases.description: Check branch naming, commit style, testing notes, changelog updates, and review expectations before opening a change.

use_cases.title: Maintain Team Documentation
use_cases.description: Update README, contribution guidance, environment notes, and release documentation with consistent project-wide language.

prompt_templates.title: Review Repository Structure
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this repository structure against the global conventions. Identify missing folders, confusing names, and documentation gaps.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare Commit and Pull Request Notes
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the global conventions to draft a commit message and pull request summary for my current changes.

prompt_templates.title: Update Environment Documentation
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the environment configuration notes and README setup steps. Suggest convention-aligned updates without exposing secret values.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Release Readiness
prompt_templates.prompt: Check this change for convention alignment across dependencies, feature flags, tests, changelog entries, and review requirements.

output_examples.input: Review my project structure for convention gaps.
output: The repository should include clear setup documentation for local development.
Dependency files are present, but update ownership notes for maintenance.
Add a contribution section that explains branch and review expectations.

output_examples.input: Prepare a pull request summary for these changes.
output: Summary: Updated setup documentation and dependency notes.
Testing: Confirmed installation steps and reviewed configuration guidance.
Review focus: Check documentation accuracy and team workflow alignment.

output_examples.input: Check environment documentation for safe wording.
output: Describe required variable names without including secret values.
Explain where developers can obtain credentials through approved channels.
Keep local examples clearly marked as placeholders.

best_practices: Use the skill before changing repository structure, workflow documentation, or release notes.
Keep secret values out of prompts, examples, documentation, and review summaries.
Pair convention guidance with project-specific human review before merging.
anti_patterns: Do not use the skill to publish real credentials or private configuration values.
Do not treat generated convention advice as automatic approval to merge.
Do not rely on this package when the referenced external conventions file is unavailable.

faq.question: What does this skill help standardize?
faq.answer: It helps standardize repository structure, Git workflow, documentation, configuration notes, dependencies, reviews, and release notes.

faq.question: Does this skill execute commands?
faq.answer: No. The reviewed package contains only a markdown instruction file and no executable scripts.

faq.question: Can it manage secrets safely?
faq.answer: It can guide safe documentation practices, but users must not provide real secret values in prompts or examples.

faq.question: Why is there a medium security warning?
faq.answer: The skill points to a conventions file outside its own package using an upward relative path.

faq.question: Is this suitable for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools.

faq.question: Does it replace team process documentation?
faq.answer: No. It helps apply conventions, but teams should maintain their own authoritative policy documents.
